en: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be uninformed.
zh: 弟兄們，關於屬靈的恩賜，我不願意你們不明白。

en: You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols, however you were led.
zh: 你們知道，你們還是教外人的時候，總是受迷惑被引誘，去拜那不能說話的偶像。

en: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking in the Spirit of God ever says "Jesus is accursed!" and no one can say "Jesus is Lord" except in the Holy Spirit.
zh: 所以我要你們知道，被 神的靈感動而說話的人，沒有一個會說“耶穌是可咒詛的”；除非是被聖靈感動，也沒有人能說“耶穌是主”。

en: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
zh: 恩賜有許多種，卻是同一位聖靈所賜的；

en: and there are varieties of service, but the same Lord;
zh: 服事的職分有許多種，但是同一位主；

en: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who empowers them all in everyone.
zh: 工作的方式也有許多種，但仍是一位 神，是他在眾人裡面作成一切。

en: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
zh: 聖靈顯現在各人的身上，為的是要使人得著益處。

en: To one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
zh: 有人藉著聖靈領受了智慧的言語，又有人靠著同一位聖靈領受了知識的言語，

en: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
zh: 又有人因著同一位聖靈領受了信心，還有人因著這位聖靈領受了醫病的恩賜，

en: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the ability to distinguish between spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
zh: 另有人可以行神蹟，另有人可以講道，另有人可以辨別諸靈，也有人能說各種的方言，也有人能翻譯方言。

en: All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit, who apportions to each one individually as he wills.
zh: 這一切都是這同一位聖靈所作的，他按照自己的意思個別地分給各人。

en: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
zh: 正如身體是一個，卻有許多肢體，而且肢體雖然很多，身體仍是一個；基督也是這樣。

en: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body- Jews or Greeks, slaves or free- and all were made to drink of one Spirit.
zh: 我們無論是猶太人，是希臘人，是作奴隸的，是自由的，都在那一位聖靈裡受了洗，成為一個身體，都飲了那一位聖靈。

en: For the body does not consist of one member but of many.
zh: 原來身體有許多肢體，並不是只有一個肢體。

en: If the foot should say, "Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body," that would not make it any less a part of the body.
zh: 假如腳說：“我不是手，所以我不屬於身體。”它不能因此就不屬於身體。

en: And if the ear should say, "Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body," that would not make it any less a part of the body.
zh: 假如耳朵說：“我不是眼睛，所以我不屬於身體。”它也不能因此就不屬於身體。

en: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the sense of hearing? If the whole body were an ear, where would be the sense of smell?
zh: 假如整個身體只是眼睛，怎樣聽呢？假如整個身體只是耳朵，怎樣聞味呢？

en: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
zh: 但現在 神按照自己的意思，把肢體一一放在身體上。

en: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
zh: 假如所有的只是一個肢體，怎能算是身體呢？

en: As it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
zh: 但現在肢體雖然很多，身體卻只是一個。

en: The eye cannot say to the hand, "I have no need of you," nor again the head to the feet, "I have no need of you."
zh: 眼睛不能對手說：“我不需要你。”頭也不能對腳說：“我不需要你們。”

en: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
zh: 相反地，身體上那些似乎比較軟弱的肢體，更是不可缺少的。

en: and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor, and our unpresentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
zh: 我們認為身體上不大體面的部分，就更加要把它裝飾得體面；不大美觀的部分，就更加要使它美觀。

en: which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body, giving greater honor to the part that lacked it,
zh: 我們身體上美觀的部分，就不需要這樣了。但 神卻這樣把身體組成了：格外地把體面加給比較有缺欠的肢體，

en: that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
zh: 好使肢體能夠互相照顧，免得身體上有了分裂。

en: If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
zh: 如果一個肢體受苦，所有的肢體就一同受苦；如果一個肢體得榮耀，所有的肢體就一同快樂。

en: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
zh: 你們就是基督的身體，並且每一個人都是作肢體的。

en: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helping, administrating, and various kinds of tongues.
zh: 神在教會裡所設立的，第一是使徒，第二是先知，第三是教師，其次是行神蹟的，再其次是有醫病恩賜的，幫助人的，治理事的，說各種方言的。

en: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
zh: 難道都是使徒嗎？都是先知嗎？都是教師嗎？都是行神蹟的嗎？

en: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
zh: 都是有醫病恩賜的嗎？都是說方言的嗎？都是翻譯方言的嗎？

en: But earnestly desire the higher gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.
zh: 你們卻要熱切地追求那些更大的恩賜。
